title: My Favourite Sport
summary: A narrator talks about his favourite sport, football, and why he loves playing it every week.
body: My favourite sport is football. I play football every Saturday morning with my friends. We go to the park near my house. There are eleven players on each team. I am a midfielder. My job is to pass the ball to the other players. I am not very fast, but I am good at passing. Before the game, we always warm up for ten minutes. We run around the field and stretch our legs. The game lasts one hour. Sometimes we win and sometimes we lose. After the game, we are very tired and very happy. We sit on the grass and drink water. Then we talk about the game together. My best friend scored three goals last week. He was so excited. I love football because it is fun and I can be with my friends. I want to play in a real match one day.

annotations.meaningVi: tiền vệ (vị trí trong bóng đá)
annotations.exampleEn: The midfielder passed the ball to the striker.
He can pass the ball very accurately.
Always warm up before you exercise.
She stretches her arms before swimming.
She scored two goals in the match.
The children were excited about the trip.
We watched the match on television.
We play in the park every weekend.
The football field is very large.
I am tired after running for one hour.

grammarNotes.title: Simple Present for habits and routines
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Thì hiện tại đơn dùng để diễn tả thói quen hoặc hoạt động lặp đi lặp lại: 'I play football every Saturday.' / 'We always warm up before the game.' Trạng từ tần suất như 'always', 'sometimes', 'every' thường đi kèm với thì này.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The simple present tense is used for habits and repeated actions: 'I play football every Saturday.' / 'We always warm up before the game.' Frequency adverbs like 'always', 'sometimes', and 'every' typically appear with this tense.

grammarNotes.title: but for contrast
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'But' dùng để nối hai ý trái ngược nhau trong cùng một câu: 'I am not very fast, but I am good at passing.' Cấu trúc này rất phổ biến ở cấp độ A1 để diễn đạt sự tương phản.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'But' connects two contrasting ideas in the same sentence: 'I am not very fast, but I am good at passing.' This structure is very common at A1 level to express contrast.

translationVi: Môn thể thao yêu thích của tôi là bóng đá. Tôi chơi bóng đá mỗi sáng thứ Bảy cùng với bạn bè. Chúng tôi đến công viên gần nhà tôi. Mỗi đội có mười một cầu thủ. Tôi là tiền vệ. Nhiệm vụ của tôi là chuyền bóng cho các cầu thủ khác. Tôi không chạy nhanh lắm, nhưng tôi giỏi chuyền bóng. Trước trận đấu, chúng tôi luôn khởi động trong mười phút. Chúng tôi chạy vòng quanh sân và duỗi chân. Trận đấu kéo dài một tiếng. Đôi khi chúng tôi thắng và đôi khi chúng tôi thua. Sau trận đấu, chúng tôi rất mệt và rất vui. Chúng tôi ngồi trên cỏ và uống nước. Sau đó chúng tôi cùng nhau nói chuyện về trận đấu. Người bạn thân nhất của tôi đã ghi ba bàn thắng tuần trước. Anh ấy rất phấn khích. Tôi yêu bóng đá vì nó vui và tôi có thể ở bên bạn bè. Tôi muốn tham gia một trận đấu thật sự một ngày nào đó.

questions.prompt: What is the narrator's favourite sport?
questions.hint: Look at the very first sentence.

questions.prompt: When does the narrator play football?
questions.explanation: 'I play football every Saturday morning with my friends.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about when he plays.

questions.prompt: Where do the friends go to play football?
options: To the park near his house
questions.explanation: 'We go to the park near my house.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about where they play.

questions.prompt: How many players are on each team?
questions.explanation: 'There are eleven players on each team.'
questions.hint: Look for the sentence about team size.

questions.prompt: What is the narrator's position in the team?
questions.hint: Find the sentence where he describes his position.

questions.prompt: What is the narrator's main job in the game?
questions.explanation: 'My job is to pass the ball to the other players.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about his role.

questions.prompt: How long do the players warm up before the game?
questions.explanation: 'We always warm up for ten minutes.'
questions.hint: Look for the sentence about warming up.

questions.prompt: How long does the game last?
questions.hint: Find the sentence about game duration.

questions.prompt: What do the players do after the game?
options: They sit on the grass and drink water
questions.explanation: 'We sit on the grass and drink water.'
questions.hint: Find what they do right after the game ends.

questions.prompt: How many goals did the narrator's best friend score last week?
questions.explanation: 'My best friend scored three goals last week.'
questions.hint: Look for the sentence about his friend's goals.

questions.prompt: Why does the narrator love football?
options: Because it is fun and he can be with his friends
questions.explanation: 'I love football because it is fun and I can be with my friends.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence that gives his reason.

questions.prompt: What does the narrator want to do one day?
questions.explanation: 'I want to play in a real match one day.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The narrator says he is not very fast but is good at passing. Do you think a team needs different types of players with different skills? Why or why not?
